programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nuns and midwives move into their new premises following the explosion that wrecked the old Nonnatus House, and are joined by fresh arrival Sister Winifred - a kindly-but-naive young woman. Jenny becomes concerned about new mother Merle's mental state and the welfare of her baby, who isn't feeding properly. Dr Turner believes it could be something more serious - although the mystery is eventually solved by the last person anyone would have expected. Chummy is struggling to adjust to her new role as a housewife and mother, and organises an open day at the community centre. Jessica Raine, Stephen McGann and Miranda Hart star(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Trixie's feathers are ruffled when Jenny is promoted, but the new Acting Sister has more to worry about after a mother-to-be reveals the baby she is carrying was not fathered by her husband - and to complicate matters, the real father is black. Cynthia is inspired by a lecture on natural childbirth, convinced it will help an overly anxious patient, while Alec and his friends try to persuade Jenny to join them on a CND march. Jessica Raine, Helen George and Bryony Hannah star in the 1950s-set nursing drama(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne receives an SOS from Holloway Prison, whose usual maternity workers have been struck down by illness, and when she and Trixie step in to help, the nun puts her reputation on the line to prevent a heavily pregnant convict having her unborn baby taken away by social services. Shelagh faces heartache when a routine pregnancy check reveals something far more serious, Alec has a confession for Jenny, and Fred takes the gang to the theatre to celebrate Chummy's birthday - only for the trip to end in disaster. Lacey Turner, best known as EastEnders' Stacey Branning, guest stars(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Jenny has an eventful few days, starting with a patient who as well as having herself to worry about, is also struggling to cope with her mother, an agoraphobic who hasn't been outside for at least 12 years. On top of that, Alec asks the midwife to accompany him to Brighton for the weekend, leaving her wondering if his intentions are strictly honourable. However, things soon take a tragic turn. Meanwhile, Shelagh joins the local choir to take her mind off her problems. Jessica Raine, Leo Staar and Laura Main star(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nuns and midwives are busy preparing for Sister Evangelina's surprise jubilee celebrations, marking 50 years since she took her vows, but a face from the past threatens to ruin the party for the guest of honour. It's back to business when a young woman with Down's syndrome is brought in feeling unwell. To everyone's surprise, they discover she is six months pregnant, causing some of them to think the worst, and the staff are joined by new nurse Patsy, whose sense of humour and no-nonsense attitude make her an instant hit(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Trixie is chuffed when local curate Tom invites her to join him for a day of cricket, sending her to work with a spring in her step. Is love on the cards for the young midwife? Shelagh's infertility has made her think long and hard about what she wants in life, so she approaches her husband with an idea, while Patsy upsets a young mother in the clinic and is moved to the district rota, where she works tirelessly to diagnose a docker's unusual symptoms(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Cynthia is concerned during a visit as she feels new mum Pamela is overly anxious, so Dr Turner arranges for a psychiatrist to come in. However, they soon discover both mother and baby have gone missing. Meanwhile, Jenny, on secondment, begins to see cracks in the establishment, Chummy discovers her mother has walked out on her father, and Trixie struggles to hide her disappointment when Tom cancels a date. Guest starring Sophie Rundle (The Bletchley Circle)(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Jenny is assigned to first-time mother Jeanette, who requires frequent visits during a protracted labour, and when she meets her patient's cousin, Philip, a new friendship begins to grow for the midwife. Meanwhile, Chummy worries about her mother's illness when she discovers Lady Browne has discharged herself from hospital. Jessica Raine and Miranda Hart star(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Robert begins a career as a journalist and Geoffrey joins a gym to help Hetty solve a case for her hairdresser, whose errant husband has a lot to hide following an apparently random attack in a gents' toilet(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Easter approaches in the spring of 1961, and Patsy helps to deliver a baby that is born with severe deformities. Her husband struggles to cope with his daughter's needs, but the sisters rally round to support the family. Trixie teaches a keep fit class which results in a medical emergency, leaving her determined to teach her patients to understand their own bodies(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Barbara treats a pregnant woman whose dockworker husband is struggling to support her as opportunities for work dry up. She is forced into a difficult decision that leaves Barbara facing the biggest test of her career. Nurse Crane meets a kindred spirit when she learns to speak Spanish, and Sister Evangelina helps a woman with breastfeeding, leading to unforeseen consequences(n)

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: A college principal is crushed to death in the shaft of a lift that has been tampered with, and suspicion falls on his widow. However, Ross believes the man's deputy was behind the fatality and sets out to prove his guilt. Meanwhile, Fraser traces his biological mother, but is disappointed by her response when he contacts her(n)

programme.desc: Lionel finishes his book and submits it to Alistair - who decides it needs a picture of the author on the cover. Unfortunately, choosing a suitable image proves troublesome. Starring Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer(n)

programme.desc: The remaining 16 contestants are split into two groups as they compete for the chance of their first taste of working in a professional kitchen. The first eight cook a dish for John Torode and Gregg Wallace, with the best five heading to Da Terra in east London to work under chef Rafael Cagali. During a busy lunch service, the amateurs will be entirely responsible for serving the restaurant's menu to paying customers. Will any buckle under the intense pressure as they cook in a demanding new environment?(n)

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: After the attack on his best friends and threats against his sister, Mobeen is armed, dangerous and out for revenge. However, his attempts to make up for past mistakes see him pulled back into the world of unhinged crime boss Uncle Khan. The return of the comedy, co-written by and starring Guz Khan, with Art Malik(n)

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Pretty Minted
programme.desc: Football vlogger Gonth decides he wants to be a TV presenter, while Mima gives up her day job as a graphic designer to risk it all to go into wedding fashion(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Ex-Manchester United defender Gary Pallister, former WBC World Super-Middleweight champion Richie Woodhall, Olympic gold medal-winning hockey player Jane Sixsmith, and former sprinter Tony Jarrett join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: London Kills
programme.desc: Grisly remains reveal the dark secrets of former student housemates. The return of the crime drama about an elite murder investigation squad in London, starring Hugo Speer, Sharon Small, Bailey Patrick and Tori Allen-Martin(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: HealthCheck UK Live
programme.desc: Daily advice for viewers in self-isolation due to the Covid-19 outbreak(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts views a ground-floor flat in London, Martel Maxwell checks out a former commercial unit in Staffordshire and Dion Dublin look at a three-bedroom semi in Birmingham(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Secret filming exposes that an insurance fraud is capable of doing all the things he claims to be unable to do, while a woman is surprised to discover her pet is not insured(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The reds and blue teams go shopping in the antique centres of Hungerford, Berkshire, joined by experts Caroline Hawley and David Harper. Eric Knowles presents and assesses their purchases(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Emma meets Jasmine's mother while Valerie looks for a choreographer. Daniel helps a young woman who worries she is pregnant as her mother plans an arranged marriage(n)

programme.title: London Kills
programme.desc: A young boy's 999 call opens a distressing case for the murder squad. Vivienne investigates stories she finds suspicious(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a former army couple find a new home in Devon on a budget of £400,000, and discovers what it takes to win pig of the year at the Devon county show(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and the team are at Wallyford Recycling Centre in East Lothian, where they transform a nest of tables, a tired trolley, and a telephone table(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson travel to the Welsh border, while silver cutlery, a croquet set, and a 110-year-old bandaged teddy bear go up for auction in Burnley(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil's return is the last thing Sharon needs on today of all days although he insists he just wants to talk to her - but when things turn violent, Ian calls the police and he is forced to vanish almost as quickly as he appeared. After a few drinks with Vinny, Dotty is clearly out to cause trouble and ends up stealing a man's wallet - only for him to give chase. Linda is caught drinking by Shirley, forcing her and Mick to make a far-reaching decision about their future(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Max anxiously awaits a reunion with her estranged daughter, but Ric and Ange are concerned that she will not be able to juggle her work commitments with this important encounter. A new nurse joins Darwin, but Jac questions whether he has the specialist skills needed to carry out the role. Essie waits to discover whether her cancer has returned, while Sacha lets Dom in on a secret - he plans to propose(n)

programme.title: Our Girl
programme.desc: Georgie helps with a polio inoculation programme and provides security for Dr Bahil, but the thought that Elvis's killer is still at large continues to consume her. She accompanies the doctor when he travels into the mountains to administer injections, and opens up to him about her painful past, leaving her questioning why she chose to come back to Afghanistan. When the mission goes wrong, Dr Bahil's life is placed in jeopardy. Prof gets a chance to prove himself, and Mimi's good intentions have worrying repercussions(n)

programme.title: My Left Nut
programme.desc: Mick is horrified to discover the whole school is talking about the bulge in his trousers - and most of them have got the wrong idea about what is causing it. While Tommy and Conor revel in the reflected glory of having a well-endowed friend, rumours start flying around about Mick's date with Rachel, which she believes he is responsible for(n)

programme.title: Peter Kay's Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: A further collection of clips documenting the much-loved comedy star's best work from his decades in showbusiness. Featuring a special appearance by The One Show's Alex Jones(n)

programme.title: London Kills
programme.desc: A young boy's 999 call opens a distressing case for the murder squad. Vivienne investigates stories she finds suspicious(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and designer Danny Clarke attempt to transform a scruffy back garden in Fife into a safe and stimulating space for an elderly man with dementia(n)

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode continues his alphabetical tour through the BBC's culinary archive, this time selecting recipes and ingredients linked by the letter C. Servings include celeriac and pomegranate salad and Raymond Blanc's cafe creme. Jamie Oliver also gives some tips(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: The programme celebrates the humble snowdrop on a visit to one of the world's largest collections, lovingly nurtured in a suburban back garden, as Monty Don offers tips on what to do once they have finished flowering. At Longmeadow it's time to get dahlias into growth and, for inspiration, there's camera footage of a field full of dahlias in Sussex along with tips from a professional grower. Plus, Nick Bailey revels in the beauty of magnolias and Monty highlights the jobs to be getting on with this weekend(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison visit Lake Vyrnwy in Powys to learn about the history of the Victorian reservoir and finding out about the birdlife that makes a home in the surrounding forests and moorland. Plus, Charlotte Smith, Tom Heap and Adam Henson joins forces for a special report on what future global trade deals could mean for UK farming and food production(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: A cutdown edition of the show featuring tips for green-fingered beginners and seasoned enthusiasts alike(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Host Susan Calman invites the two highest-scoring London and the South-east chefs to go head to head and cook their six-course menus again. The duo need to impress formidable judges Matthew Fort, Oliver Peyton and Andi Oliver as well as guest judge, the one-time Blue Peter presenter Konnie Huq, whose Cookie and the Most Annoying Boy in the World adventure series is making waves with young readers(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein: From Venice to Istanbul
programme.desc: The chef's gastronomic road trip reaches a conclusion as he arrives in Istanbul, just in time for the bluefish season, which allows him the chance to enjoy a fishing expedition on the Bosphorus, one of the world's most strategic waterways, which divides the city into two continents. Rick also cooks two much-loved local dishes - lavish kofte kebabs with pistachios, and kapuska, a hearty traditional Turkish cuisine stew from the Black Sea region, whose name is derived from the Russian word for cabbage(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The remarkable story of Sudan, the very last male northern white rhino on the planet. Snatched from his mother's side in Central Africa, Sudan became a prized exhibit in a zoo while the rest of his kind were poached to near-extinction. This programme focuses on the desperate battle to bring his sub-species back from the brink of extinction, told through the characters who have been involved in his life(n)

programme.title: Mexico: Earth's Festival of Life
programme.desc: The first of three programmes about the wildlife, landscape and culture in different areas of the country explores the Sierra Madre mountains, and reveals how this rugged landscape has shaped the nation. This chain of peaks includes several volcanoes and is home to a wide variety of animals, including black bears, monarch butterflies, orchid bees and quetzals. The programme also visits the ruins of an ancient Aztec shrine and the blue agave plant which grows in the mountains is used in making tequila(n)

programme.title: Evil Under the Sun (1982)
programme.desc: Hercule Poirot braves the heat and sand to investigate the murder of a glamorous stage star on an idyllic Adriatic holiday island. It becomes clear many people had reason to want the temperamental actress dead and the Belgian sleuth is soon hard at work figuring out which of her enemies eventually managed to kill her. Mystery, with Peter Ustinov, Diana Rigg, James Mason, Maggie Smith and Jane Birkin(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction, and Paul Martin finds out about the history of Sledmere House in East Yorkshire(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Arden family experience corner shop life in the 1980s and 1990s, when stores changed from primary shopping destinations to a place for last minute impulse buys and were forced to make the maximum use of their floor space to stock thousands of products. The programme examines the impact of new legislation allowing supermarkets to open on Sundays, previously the only advantage small businesses had over them. ABC's Martin Fry visits the shop and reveals how the band used to use empty industrial units as cheap rehearsal space, and there is a look at the rise of the gaming industry and a revival of a famous 1980s marketing stunt(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon reaches the United States, travelling down the Rocky Mountains from Montana to Colorado. In the wide-open prairies of Montana, the cowboy way of life is under threat from a long-term decline in the rural economy, but Simon meets a former Silicon Valley executive who has envisaged a new future for the land. In a country with the highest incarceration rate in the world, Simon's final destination on this leg is a Colorado town that depends almost entirely on 11 prisons. Simon witnesses how inmates are put to work for the state, and how virtual reality is being used to prepare prisoners for life on the outside(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams venture through Peru's high altitudes as the race reaches the halfway mark - but the first hurdle they face is bypassing the ongoing state of emergency in Ecuador, and drastic measures are required to ensure the competitors' safety. Emon and Jamiul are in the lead but massively over budget, so try to get work with some local fishermen, while Dom and Lizzie discover a connection to their grandmother in Peru's capital(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Sara Davies, Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman and Tej Lalvani consider the profit-making potential of more business ideas. A German-Portuguese partnership proposes a new kind of camera lens for mobile phones, a lifelong baker shares her recipe for vegan treats, and a third team pitches their idea for intravenous vitamins. Evan Davis presents(n)

programme.title: Trigonometry
programme.desc: It is the day of Gemma and Kieran's wedding, but the happy couple are still reeling from Ray's heartfelt declaration the night before, and struggle to cope with their feelings. Kieran lashes out, while Ray tries to avoid drawing attention to herself - until Gemma's friends drag her into helping them get the bride ready(n)

programme.title: Jawbone (2017)
programme.desc: After hitting rock bottom, former youth boxing champion Jimmy McCabe returns to his childhood boxing club and his old team - gym owner Bill, corner-man Eddie and promoter Joe. Back in training, years after anyone thought he was a contender, he risks his life as he tries to stand tall and regain his place in the world. Drama, starring Johnny Harris, Ray Winstone, Ian McShane and Michael Smiley(n)

programme.title: The Homesman (2014)
programme.desc: A single woman living in a frontier town is asked by her church to escort three women traumatised by the hardships of their lives to a safe refuge in the East. Along the way, she saves an outlaw's life and persuades him to help her protect her charges on the perilous journey through the wilderness. Western based on the novel by Glendon Swarthout, directed by and starring Tommy Lee Jones, with Hilary Swank, Meryl Streep and Miranda Otto(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Shrewsbury, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions send in by viewers on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: Investigators uncover a senior manager gambling with an NHS credit card, and an exotic dance troupe boss goes missing after defrauding the public purse of thousands of pounds(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Xavier is piecing together the puzzle of Evan's death and getting dangerously close to exposing the truth. How far will Cam go to neutralise him? Ben deals with Roger Ffolkes, who forces him to face some difficult facts, while Jac is keen to impress the Board with a complex and rare surgery that will put them ahead of the competition(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore searches for tired and unwanted items at Woking Recycling Centre in Surrey she can transform from skip-bound to stylish. With three items saved, Sarah turns to upholsterer Sharon O'Connor to overhaul an old chair frame, while upcycler Jacqui Joseph attempts to transform a dated desk. Sarah's challenge is to update a set of four chairs. Narrated by Arthur Smith(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams at the Peterborough Festival of Antiques find themselves in the capable hands of experts Stephanie Connell and Philip Serrell, trying to bag items they can make a profit on at auction. As well as monitoring proceedings, presenter Eric Knowles has an intriguing mystery to solve(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman presents the semi-final, which sees the four remaining cooks produce a selection of party canapes and rustle up a dish from a tinned store cupboard staple. The ones who impress the least must fight for their place in the final by cooking again in the eliminator - and this week, Angela Hartnett gets them to produce a recipe also happens to be her husband's favourite dessert. Who will be hanging up their apron at the last hurdle?(n)

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, with David Cornock(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The team focuses on the Irish Sea and its surrounding land, with Nick Crane finding out about a unique seasonal phenomenon of this sea - a current called 'the gyre' which provides the perfect environment for Dublin Bay prawns to flourish. Mark Horton explores the archaeological discovery of two Roman temples and an early Christian church in Maryport, across the water from Dublin, and Tessa Dunlop reveals how one small yacht changed the course of British and Irish history. Cassie Newland and Miranda Krestovnikoff tell the tale of RMS Tayleur, a ship that sank on its maiden voyage with a great loss of life, and singer Ruth Keggin tells the story of the Irish sea god Mannanan(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Eric Knowles takes on Danny Sebastian at an antiques fair in Nottinghamshire, with the two taking very different approaches to buying and selling(n)

programme.title: The TV That Made Me
programme.desc: Brian Conley invites Pam Ayres to pick the TV shows that shaped her life, with the poet's selection including one of the scariest series ever broadcast on British TV(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Supt Kevin Dunne receives the prime suspect treatment, while Ash and a reluctant Paudge seek a missing witness to Donna Burke's fate(n)

programme.title: Alex Polizzi: Chefs on Trial
programme.desc: The best three chefs from the week face their final challenge - to produce a three-course fine-dining menu for a restaurant full of customers, as well as managing a team of chefs who will be testing not only their cooking skills but also their ability to run a kitchen. At the end of the evening, the owners of The Gilpin and Alex Polizzi decide who will get the job(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Raj Bisram assess items brought to the Old College in Aberystwyth, while presenter Paul Martin visits a Regency restoration project at the National Botanic Garden of Wales(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Susie Dent, Nick Owen, Chemmy Alcott and Dane Baptiste go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle are at Longleat Safari Park, getting ready to welcome five Southern koalas that are en route from Adelaide in Australia. Jean Johansson is in the wolf wood to see if the European grey wolves can live up to their reputation for having one of the best senses of smell in the animal world(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Mediterranean Adventure
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers get back on their bikes to explore the culinary delights of several Mediterranean countries, discovering hearty and healthy food from France, Spain and Italy. They begin by searching for the authentic flavours of Italy, from simple local ingredients to spicy nduja pate and some of the best gelato around(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell asks the questions in the final of the quiz, with two teams battling it out to succeed last year's winners the Dicers and be named champions. The teams must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between groups of four things that may at first appear not to be linked, with one set comprising Take On Me, Super Mario 64, Voyage of the Dawn Treader, and Mary Poppins(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The quarter-finals conclude, with two teams of four students battling it out to make it to the next stage of the competition as they try to win a second match and progress to the semi-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: The Real Michael Jackson
programme.desc: Jacques Peretti investigates the pop star's character, going back to his beginnings, charting his rise and fall and seeks a fuller picture of this complex, contradictory man by exploring what clues were missed. He examines how Jackson became what he was, looks at whether he manipulated his fame and the entertainment machine to conceal the truth, and explores what the audience's part was in it all(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Sheep farmers head to Ryedale to show off the finest animals in their flocks, including a retired businessman who is hoping to carry on his wife's legacy(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison visit Lake Vyrnwy in Powys to learn about the history of the Victorian reservoir and finding out about the birdlife that makes a home in the surrounding forests and moorland. Plus, Charlotte Smith, Tom Heap and Adam Henson joins forces for a special report on what future global trade deals could mean for UK farming and food production(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Officers raid an illegal sweat shop in Birmingham, and pursue a builder who rendered a family home unfit to be lived in and its owners £17,000 out of pocket(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades transforms a stained-glass door and window, Rachel Henderson takes an old fireplace, and Mark Haig attempts to inject new life into a collection of brass lighting(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms, and the memories they hold, back to life. The team set to work repairing a Jamaican pump organ, an RAF bomber pilot's hat, a naval rigging tool kit, and a crib made from wood salvaged from a church that was bombed during the First World War(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The team explores nature at its most perilous on the wild waters of Britain's coast. Nick Crane reveals why the first lifeboat station was founded on the north-east coast at Bamburgh Castle, Mark Horton finds out how ferries cope with stormy seas, and Miranda Krestovnikoff explores the jellyfish of Dublin Bay - and how both swimmers and scientists are seeking an antidote to its excruciating sting. Dick Strawbridge investigates one of the strangest stories of the Second World War - did the Germans really invade the south coast in 1940?(n)

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Appeals for funds on behalf of a registered charity(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Kate Bliss takes on Caroline Hawley at an auction in Kent and veteran Eric Knowles challenges rookie Chuko Ojiri at a car boot sale in Chesterfield(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Bridget and Vincent are pawns in an imminent atrocity. The accomplice in Donna's disposal is exposed(n)

programme.title: Alex Polizzi: Chefs on Trial
programme.desc: The troubleshooter tackles Amelies, a harbour-side seafood restaurant in the picturesque Cornish town of Porthleven. Owner Sam Sheffield Dunstan gave up a high-flying career in London five years ago to fulfil a lifetime ambition of opening an eatery. However, her dream turned into a nightmare as she failed to attract the right head chef, so a desperate Sam turned to Alex to help find the best person for the job. In this edition, Alex puts the first candidates through their paces(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin hosts from Rochester Cathedral in Kent, where antiques expert Elizabeth Talbot falls for a book of fairy tales illustrated by Arthur Rackham, and Adam Partridge discovers an elegant chiming Swiss pocket watch. Paul also meets one of the last remaining survivors of the Dunkirk evacuation during the Second World War(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Susie Dent, Nick Owen, Chemmy Alcott and Dane Baptiste test their general knowledge skills in the quiz, all hoping they stand a chance of winning Friday's final(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle follow a male lion being introduced to eight females in the hope of starting a whole new pride in the park. Plus, how elephants keep maintain the strength of their trunks in captivity and an attempt to mate two emus(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams venture through Peru's high altitudes as the race reaches the halfway mark - but the first hurdle they face is bypassing the ongoing state of emergency in Ecuador, and drastic measures are required to ensure the competitors' safety. Emon and Jamiul are in the lead but massively over budget, so try to get work with some local fishermen, while Dom and Lizzie discover a connection to their grandmother in Peru's capital(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Arderns reflect on the lessons learned through the series, and how corner shops can continue to thrive in the changing world of 21st-century business. The programme examines how shops have reinvented themselves to compete with mini-supermarkets, the rise of zero-waste shops, and how other businesses, like cafes, coffee shops and micro pubs, are filling the role of community hub(n)

programme.title: Cops Like Us
programme.desc: The Staffordshire Police officers look to the future as they take on new recruits, including trainee Holly Swift. After working in mental health services and seeing the difference police officers can make, she decided it was the career for her. At the other end of the career path is Sgt Dave Allan - who is just about to complete 30 years' service and retire. The police service recruited heavily after the miners' strikes in the 1980s, and many of those officers are now approaching retirement, including Dave(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Tom Allen, Ed Gamble, Kerry Godliman, Rhys James and Rosie Jones and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. As usual, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The documentary looks at Alder Hey Children's Hospital's craniofacial department - one of only four in the UK that treats life-threatening congenital skull and facial abnormalities. With a rise in referrals, the department is over-subscribed and neurosurgeon Chris Parks, who leads the craniofacial surgical team, faces a non-stop workload. Chris always works hand in glove with Ben Robertson, one of three paediatric maxillofacial surgeons in the UK. But this partnership is about to be broken up, as Ben is leaving to return to his native Australia. It takes 23 years' training to become a maxillofacial Consultant specialising in cranio-surgery, and there is currently nobody experienced enough to replace Ben, putting extra pressure on the waiting lists(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Port Lockroy on Goudier Island in the British Antarctic Territory is the site of one of the world's most remote post offices, which has a staff of four and is open to visiting tourists during the Antarctic summer. They share the neighbourhood with more than 3,000 gentoo penguins, and this documentary follows the trials and tribulations of the birds as they try to raise their chicks in these unusual surroundings. Juliet Stevenson narrates(n)

programme.title: Britain's Lost Waterlands: Escape to Swallows and Amazons Country
programme.desc: Presenters Dick Strawbridge and Professor Alice Roberts explore the British landscapes that inspired children's author Arthur Ransome to write his famous series Swallows and Amazons. Strawbridge uses vintage boats to investigate three iconic waterlands - the Lake District, the Norfolk broads, and the Suffolk coastline - while Roberts takes a look at the shore lines(n)

programme.title: Operation Gold Rush with Dan Snow
programme.desc: Dan Snow, Felicity Aston and Dr Kevin Fong build a wooden boat, following original designs for the vessels used during the Gold Rush. This vessel must carry them across hundreds of miles of treacherous lakes and rivers, including the infamous One Mile Rapids, a narrow canyon of white water and jagged rocks(n)

programme.title: The Odyssey (2016)
programme.desc: Biopic covering 30 years in the life of acclaimed oceanographer and filmmaker Jacques-Yves Cousteau. Starring Lambert Wilson, Pierre Niney and Audrey Tautou. In French(n)

programme.title: Francis Bacon: A Brush with Violence
programme.desc: Documentary exploring just how much of Francis Bacon's notoriety as a rude, drunken loudmouth was a carefully curated front he maintained to protect the complex and haunted man behind his own myth. Making use of interviews with celebrity commentators, fellow artists, and some of the people who knew him best, this programme sets out to deconstruct the perceived and hidden identities of the man known as one of the most sought-after British artists of the 20th century(n)

programme.title: Handmade in Bolton
programme.desc: Historian Dr Janina Ramirez and reformed forger Shaun Greenhalgh work to recreate ancient works of art, beginning with a jeweled eagle brooch fit for a Visigoth chieftain. They visit an animal sanctuary in Lancashire to draw inspiration a close-up encounter with a bird of prey - but sourcing authentic materials proves tricky(n)

programme.title: Discovery Concerts
programme.desc: Katie Derham introduces a performance by the BBC Concert Orchestra of the full score to David Lean's 1957 film. She talks to film composer Debbie Wiseman about what makes it so special, both as a piece in its own right and as an element of the film, and discovers the story of the composer's troubled life, from his committed pacifism during the Second World War to his later struggles with mental illness and alcoholism(n)

programme.title: Britain's Lost Waterlands: Escape to Swallows and Amazons Country
programme.desc: Presenters Dick Strawbridge and Professor Alice Roberts explore the British landscapes that inspired children's author Arthur Ransome to write his famous series Swallows and Amazons. Strawbridge uses vintage boats to investigate three iconic waterlands - the Lake District, the Norfolk broads, and the Suffolk coastline - while Roberts takes a look at the shore lines(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Francesco's Mediterranean Voyage
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto pays his respects to the soldiers commemorated at the war cemeteries of Gallipoli, monument to the Dardanelles campaign of World War One. In Edirne, he views Turkey's largest mosque and the second-oldest recognised sporting event after the Olympics, while Bursa reveals the origins of the doner kebab(n)

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates footage of painted wolves on the banks of the Zambezi river, as an ageing matriarch faces a challenge for supremacy from her own daughter, who has grown to lead a pack of her own, which now needs to expand into new territory. With human-occupied land penning them in, the two packs are forced to fight for the little resources available(n)

programme.title: Scandal & Beauty: Mark Gatiss on Aubrey Beardsley
programme.desc: The actor and writer explores the life of the Victorian artist who scandalised the art world of the 1890s, revealing a man who was a technological innovator and whose uncompromising attitude still feels remarkably. He follows Beardsley's footsteps from his childhood in Brighton, via notoriety among the decadence of London's fin de siècle, to his early death in France in 1898 at the age of just 25(n)

programme.title: England's Forgotten Queen: The Life and Death of Lady Jane Grey
programme.desc: Helen Castor concludes the docudrama telling the story of the woman who reigned England and Ireland for nine days in 1553. The historian looks at the final days of her reign, when she had been installed in the Tower of London, only for Mary Tudor's forces to assemble ready for battle. And when one of Jane's key's supporters defected to Mary's side, taking thousands of supporters, the balance tipped and the men who installed her on the throne abandoned her(n)

programme.title: Sex and Sensibility: The Allure of Art Nouveau
programme.desc: Stephen Smith explores how the development of the 'jugendstil' aesthetic revolutionised the art and architecture of Vienna in the late 19th and early 20th centuries. Through the work of artists and designers including Gustav Klimt, Josef Hoffmann, Koloman Moser and Egon Schiele, he explains how Viennese disciples of art nouveau rebelled against establishment ideas and sought to make their art socially relevant(n)

programme.title: Christo and Jeanne-Claude: Monumental Art
programme.desc: Documentary which charts the creation of world-renowned artist Christo's first public work of art in the UK, the Mastaba. In the summer of 2018, on the Serpentine in London's Hyde Park, Christo constructed the Mastaba from 7,506 painted oil barrels. The work was inspired by ancient Mesopotamian tombs and weighs six hundred tonnes. It is the latest work in a career spanning half a century and stretching across the world, and the programme looks back on Christo's career and a life spent making provocative works of art with his wife and partner Jeanne-Claude(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of Books
programme.desc: Actress and director Fiona Shaw explores the genesis of George Eliot's classic 1860 novel, the plot of which had parallels with the author's own life(n)

programme.title: Ben Building: Mussolini, Monuments and Modernism
programme.desc: Jonathan Meades travels to Rome, Milan, Genoa and the new town of Sabaudia as he turns his attention to the notorious 20th century European dictator, Mussolini. He examines the variety of styles of buildings of the fascist era that still shape architecture today, and considers the nature of fascism, the influence of The Futurists and Mussolini's love of a uniform(n)

programme.title: Scandal & Beauty: Mark Gatiss on Aubrey Beardsley
programme.desc: The actor and writer explores the life of the Victorian artist who scandalised the art world of the 1890s, revealing a man who was a technological innovator and whose uncompromising attitude still feels remarkably. He follows Beardsley's footsteps from his childhood in Brighton, via notoriety among the decadence of London's fin de siècle, to his early death in France in 1898 at the age of just 25(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world. With Katty Kay and Christian Fraser(n)

programme.title: Gareth Edwards' Great Welsh Adventure
programme.desc: Rugby legend Gareth and wife Maureen reach the final leg of their round-Wales adventure by canal, one which has a personal connection for them. The narrowboat novices grew up at opposite ends of the same street in the village of Gwaun-cae-Gurwen, near Neath. As children, they would play in the areas by the derelict canals, but knew very little of them and their history. The Swansea canal and the Neath and Tennant canals reveal a story of industry that once dominated the area, getting coal and iron to the docks. Now virtually impassable, there are plans afoot to give them a new lease of life(n)

programme.title: Digging for Britain: The Greatest Discoveries
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts re-examines key archaeological sites of prehistoric Britain(n)

programme.title: The Art Mysteries
programme.desc: Waldemar Januszczak examines Gauguin's Vision After the Sermon,an epic 17th-century religious painting exploring temptation and desire. He uncovers some unusual connections with sumo wrestling, Victor Hugo's Les Miserables, and a 17-year-old girl called Madeleine(n)

programme.title: Britain Beneath Your Feet
programme.desc: First in a two-part documentary in which Dallas Campbell takes a unique look at Britain, exploring the hidden wonders beneath people's feet. Along the way he finds out how the Shard of London stays standing on soft clay and reveals how Edinburgh was set upon an ancient volcano. The presenter also abseils down an underground waterfall higher than Niagara, canoes along a secret river under the city of Bristol and discovers a vast root system beneath one of the nation's oldest oak trees(n)

programme.title: Britain's Ancient Capital: Secrets of Orkney
programme.desc: An investigation into how recent archaeological finds on the archipelago indicate that Orkney was the cultural capital of Stone Age Britain and the birthplace of the stone circle cult. Chris Packham uncovers the secrets revealed by the DNA of Orkney's unique vole, Neil Oliver explores tombs and monuments, Shini Somara experiments to discover how ancient Orcadians could have moved giant blocks of stone over rough ground, and Andy Torbet climbs a challenging sea-stack to unlock the story of Orkney's unusual geology(n)

programme.title: Soup Cans & Superstars: How Pop Art Changed the World
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke examines the history of the art movement, bringing a fresh eye to the work of superstars Andy Warhol and Roy Lichtenstein and tracking down pioneers including James Rosenquist, Claes Oldenburg, Ed Ruscha, Peter Blake and Allen Jones. He also explores pop art's fascination with celebrity, advertising and the mass media, and travels to China to discover how a new generation of artists is reinventing it for the 21st century(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet Revisited

programme.title: Countryfile

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return for a second visit to Morden Hall Park in South London, where items include letters written by Mother Teresa and a bronze by South African sculptor Anton Van Wouw. There is also a colourful collage by celebrated British artist and illustrator John Minton, and a huge collection of intricately designed silver vinaigrette boxes(n)

programme.title: The Nest
programme.desc: Three months have passed and Kaya is living with Dan and Emily and enjoying her new lifestyle, but James has his reservations and is concerned that she is being exploited. Souter is suspicious Kaya's background doesn't quite check out and is having to field questions from young and ambitious journalist Eleanor. At home tensions between the couple and their surrogate are at breaking point, and Dan questions what he really knows about the teenager he has invited into his home. Thriller, starring Martin Compston, Sophie Rundle and Mirren Mack(n)

programme.title: Rain Man (1988)
programme.desc: Brash yuppie car dealer Charlie discovers his deceased father has left the family fortune to Raymond, the autistic elder brother he never knew he had. In a bid to get his hands on the money, he tracks down and abducts Raymond - but the ensuing cross-country journey proves to be a profound learning experience. Oscar-winning drama, starring Tom Cruise and Dustin Hoffman, with Valeria Golino, Gerald R Molen and Jack Murdock(n)

programme.title: Panorama

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The remaining 16 contestants are split into two groups as they compete for the chance of their first taste of working in a professional kitchen. The first eight cook a dish for John Torode and Gregg Wallace, with the best five heading to Da Terra in east London to work under chef Rafael Cagali. During a busy lunch service, the amateurs will be entirely responsible for serving the restaurant's menu to paying customers. Will any buckle under the intense pressure as they cook in a demanding new environment?(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil's return is the last thing Sharon needs on today of all days although he insists he just wants to talk to her - but when things turn violent, Ian calls the police and he is forced to vanish almost as quickly as he appeared. After a few drinks with Vinny, Dotty is clearly out to cause trouble and ends up stealing a man's wallet - only for him to give chase. Linda is caught drinking by Shirley, forcing her and Mick to make a far-reaching decision about their future(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Max anxiously awaits a reunion with her estranged daughter, but Ric and Ange are concerned that she will not be able to juggle her work commitments with this important encounter. A new nurse joins Darwin, but Jac questions whether he has the specialist skills needed to carry out the role. Essie waits to discover whether her cancer has returned, while Sacha lets Dom in on a secret - he plans to propose(n)

programme.title: Our Girl
programme.desc: Georgie helps with a polio inoculation programme and provides security for Dr Bahil, but the thought that Elvis's killer is still at large continues to consume her. She accompanies the doctor when he travels into the mountains to administer injections, and opens up to him about her painful past, leaving her questioning why she chose to come back to Afghanistan. When the mission goes wrong, Dr Bahil's life is placed in jeopardy. Prof gets a chance to prove himself, and Mimi's good intentions have worrying repercussions(n)

programme.title: My Left Nut
programme.desc: Mick is horrified to discover the whole school is talking about the bulge in his trousers - and most of them have got the wrong idea about what is causing it. While Tommy and Conor revel in the reflected glory of having a well-endowed friend, rumours start flying around about Mick's date with Rachel, which she believes he is responsible for(n)

programme.title: Peter Kay's Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: A further collection of clips documenting the much-loved comedy star's best work from his decades in showbusiness. Featuring a special appearance by The One Show's Alex Jones(n)

programme.desc: A young boy's 999 call opens a distressing case for the murder squad. Vivienne investigates stories she finds suspicious(n)

programme.title: Madagascar 3: Europe's Most Wanted (2012)
programme.desc: The escaped zoo animals continue their quest to get back to their New York home. Animated comedy, with the voices of Ben Stiller, Chris Rock, David Schwimmer and Jada Pinkett Smith(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Siblings from Wolverhampton are paired with chefs Ellis Barrie and Romy Gill. Sommer and Daryl's large family gatherings revolve around shared meals and they were both taught to cook by their grandmother, who cheers them on from the audience. Fiercely competitive, Sommer intends to beat her little brother, but Daryl is determined he can get the better of his sister. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet Revisited
programme.desc: In the first of two programmes, Steve Backshall discovers why the Bahamas provide a sanctuary for sharks, looking at the protection offered to them, people's changing attitudes and the key role science plays in their conservation. He joins researchers who have dedicated their lives to this unique and often misunderstood group of animals and learns that a healthy shark population is absolutely vital to a healthy marine ecosystem(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison visits the Lugg Valley in Herefordshire to hunt for signs of spring, joining volunteers making sure toads can make it safely across main roads to their favoured spawning grounds. programme.desc: Grisly remains reveal the dark secrets of former student housemates. The return of the crime drama about an elite murder investigation squad in London, starring Hugo Speer, Sharon Small, Bailey Patrick and Tori Allen-Martin(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a south London couple find a new home in the High Weald in south-east England, and also visits a wildlife reserve in the Kent countryside(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades transforms a stained-glass door and window, Rachel Henderson takes an old fireplace, and Mark Haig attempts to inject new life into a collection of brass lighting(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Christina Trevanion set out from Morecambe to hunt for items to sell at an auction in Sheffield(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Bake host the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Documentary looking at life in modern-day Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: It's the day of Dennis's funeral but when Jay arrives with flowers from Ben and Lola, Sharon insists that none of the Mitchells are welcome. Meanwhile, Dotty piles on the pressure for a guilty Ian to come clean about his part in the young man's death. Linda refuses to let Shirley dampen her progress and tells Mick she is ready to return to work full-time. Dotty strikes up a friendship with Vinny, and Habiba and Jags have a heart-to-heart(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Current affairs report(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The remaining 16 contestants are split into two groups as they compete for the chance of their first taste of working in a professional kitchen. The first eight cook a dish for John Torode and Gregg Wallace, with the best five heading to Da Terra in east London to work under chef Rafael Cagali. During a busy lunch service, the amateurs will be entirely responsible for serving the restaurant's menu to paying customers. Will any buckle under the intense pressure as they cook in a demanding new environment?(n)

programme.title: My First Home
programme.desc: Vinny Hurrell presents a programme following people who are attempting to get on the property ladder, with interior designer Ian Thompson joining the potential buyers to visit three properties and a dream house for inspiration. Returning to the studio, a virtual makeover of their chosen house is revealed, to give them an idea of what can be done for their budget(n)

programme.title: BBC Arts NI Presents
programme.desc: Stephen McCauley presents this show providing a platform for emerging homegrown musical talent, including Jordan Adetunji, JC Stewart, Wild Youth, New Pagans and Dena Anuksa(n)

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: After the attack on his best friends and threats against his sister, Mobeen is armed, dangerous and out for revenge. However, his attempts to make up for past mistakes see him pulled back into the world of unhinged crime boss Uncle Khan. The return of the comedy, co-written by and starring Guz Khan, with Art Malik(n)

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Pretty Minted
programme.desc: Football vlogger Gonth decides he wants to be a TV presenter, while Mima gives up her day job as a graphic designer to risk it all to go into wedding fashion(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Ex-Manchester United defender Gary Pallister, former WBC World Super-Middleweight champion Richie Woodhall, Olympic gold medal-winning hockey player Jane Sixsmith, and former sprinter Tony Jarrett join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: HealthCheck UK Live
programme.desc: Daily advice for viewers in self-isolation due to the Covid-19 outbreak(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts views a ground-floor flat in London, Martel Maxwell checks out a former commercial unit in Staffordshire and Dion Dublin look at a three-bedroom semi in Birmingham(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Secret filming exposes that an insurance fraud is capable of doing all the things he claims to be unable to do, while a woman is surprised to discover her pet is not insured(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The reds and blue teams go shopping in the antique centres of Hungerford, Berkshire, joined by experts Caroline Hawley and David Harper. Eric Knowles presents and assesses their purchases(n)

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.desc: Emma meets Jasmine's mother while Valerie looks for a choreographer. Daniel helps a young woman who worries she is pregnant as her mother plans an arranged marriage(n)

programme.title: London Kills
programme.desc: A young boy's 999 call opens a distressing case for the murder squad. Vivienne investigates stories she finds suspicious(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country

programme.title: Money for Nothing

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson travel to the Welsh border, while silver cutlery, a croquet set, and a 110-year-old bandaged teddy bear go up for auction in Burnley(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil's return is the last thing Sharon needs on today of all days although he insists he just wants to talk to her - but when things turn violent, Ian calls the police and he is forced to vanish almost as quickly as he appeared. After a few drinks with Vinny, Dotty is clearly out to cause trouble and ends up stealing a man's wallet - only for him to give chase. Linda is caught drinking by Shirley, forcing her and Mick to make a far-reaching decision about their future(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Max anxiously awaits a reunion with her estranged daughter, but Ric and Ange are concerned that she will not be able to juggle her work commitments with this important encounter. A new nurse joins Darwin, but Jac questions whether he has the specialist skills needed to carry out the role. Essie waits to discover whether her cancer has returned, while Sacha lets Dom in on a secret - he plans to propose(n)

programme.title: Our Girl
programme.desc: Georgie helps with a polio inoculation programme and provides security for Dr Bahil, but the thought that Elvis's killer is still at large continues to consume her. She accompanies the doctor when he travels into the mountains to administer injections, and opens up to him about her painful past, leaving her questioning why she chose to come back to Afghanistan. When the mission goes wrong, Dr Bahil's life is placed in jeopardy. Prof gets a chance to prove himself, and Mimi's good intentions have worrying repercussions(n)

programme.title: My Left Nut
programme.desc: Mick is horrified to discover the whole school is talking about the bulge in his trousers - and most of them have got the wrong idea about what is causing it. While Tommy and Conor revel in the reflected glory of having a well-endowed friend, rumours start flying around about Mick's date with Rachel, which she believes he is responsible for(n)

programme.title: Dràgonan - Rèis chun an iomaill (Dragons: Race to the Edge)
programme.desc: Part one of two. The Defenders of the Wing are a local island tribe, which worships dragons and believes the Riders have stolen its protector - the Eruptodon dragon(n)

programme.title: Vets: Gach Creutair Beo
programme.desc: Young Wick vets Kiri Harvey and Pete Cameron are kept busy with lambing and calving, and have some difficult decisions to make. A sheep heavy with triplets comes in and it's a race against time to see if the lambs can be born alive. At Ardene House near Aberdeen, visiting vet Fergus Coutts uses acupuncture and hydrotherapy to treat a German Shepherd dog, while a cat with an abscess on his head is admitted as an emergency case for vet David Mackenzie to treat(n)

programme.title: Beul Chainnt
programme.desc: Eilidh MacLeod explores the richness and diversity of the Gaelic language, delving into subjects as diverse as churches, boat building and children's games(n)

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news reports(n)

programme.title: Fuine (Home Baking)
programme.desc: Gregor MacLeod and his trusted bakers and cooks reveal the recipes they turn to when on a strict budget. Gregor visits his neighbour in Lewis who shows him a great way to use up mashed potato by making sweet and chocolatey macaroons with it. He then heads to Kyle of Lochalsh, where an accomplished cook shows him how she uses tinned salmon to make a delicious tart. Back at home, Gregor puts old bananas to good use in his sticky caramel upside-down cake(n)

programme.title: Clann Pheter Roraidh
programme.desc: The story of the Campbell family from the Isle of Barra. Tony Kearney is a well-known Scottish actor and broadcaster, but his family's talents extend back generations. The programme features the children of Peter Rory, Tony's late grandfather, as cameras witness how their creative legacy lives on(n)

programme.title: Fraochy Bay

programme.title: Togaidh Sinn Fonn (Join in the Music)

programme.title: Alleluia! (Spiritual Music & Verse)

programme.title: Ainsley's Mediterranean Cookbook
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott's tour of Corsica brings him to the island of Bonifacio, where he learns about its history and culture from tour guide Christine, before whipping up some cheesy stuffed aubergines. He also visits a honey producer, joins a local chef to cook with bovine bread and meets an olive oil-maker, using his some for his fish stew with garlic mayonnaise(n)

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The expert walker chooses her favourite, most accessible family treks in search of the perfect walk with a view that anyone can tackle in a morning or afternoon ramble, venturing off the beaten track and discovering some of Britain's most breath-taking scenery(n)

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis presents highlights from the spinning-chair singing contest, Revisiting the coaches' favourite Blind Auditions and featuring some of the most memorable performances(n)

programme.title: Ant & Dec's Saturday Night Takeaway
programme.desc: The popular duo's entertainment show, featuring undercover pranks, surprises galore, star guests and a chance for a member of the audience to win the items advertised in one of last week's commercial breaks(n)

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show from locations around the UK, setting members of the public unusual challenges for a chance to win anything from the price of their petrol, to £1000 in the big finale game. This week, Stephen is in Nottingham(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard invites former footballer David Ginola, broadcaster Janet Street-Porter and reality TV star Chris Hughes to take part in this celebrity edition of the quiz show, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine, winning cash with each successful drop(n)

programme.title: Star Wars: Episode VII - The Force Awakens (2015)
programme.desc: A stormtrooper fighting for an evil regime turns against his masters. He joins forces with a desert scavenger, and together they are drawn into the search for legendary war hero Luke Skywalker, who disappeared years previously. Meanwhile, a terrible weapon is about to be unleashed, that will allow the forces of evil to dominate the galaxy once again. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Daisy Ridley, John Boyega, Harrison Ford and Adam Driver(n)

programme.title: The Family Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents the special edition of the game show in which four relatives team up - this week the Marshes from Bolton - and pit their wits against one of the finest quiz brains in the country, in the hope of taking away thousands of pounds(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: Gabriel and Aisha get closer when they work on a surgery together, threatening Ruby and Gabriel's blossoming relationship. Ruby and Ram fall out over a patient's care, while Edmund's search for his lost love hits a dead end. Medical drama, starring Amanda Redman, Amrita Acharia, James Krishna Floyd and Darshan Jariwalla, with a guest appearance by Kenneth Cranham(n)

programme.title: Belgravia
programme.desc: Anne and James are at loggerheads following the revelations at the Brockenhursts' party. With Oliver fixated on discovering the origins of his father's new protege, Susan uses the opportunity to plan a covert trip to meet with a new friend, and enlists her maid Speer to keep the true nature of the rendezvous under wraps. Meanwhile, Lady Templemore begins work on her lavish plans for her daughter's wedding, but much to her disappointment, Lady Maria seems fixated on someone other than her fiance(n)

programme.title: The Station: Trouble on the Tracks
programme.desc: The rail mobile detection team discovers a high-tech home - complete with electricity, running water and TV - illegally erected under an arch beside one of the country's busiest areas of track. At Euston, Transport Police officers are called upon when tempers flare - and deal with an unusual Royal request. Meanwhile, a strike hits Birmingham's New Street Station and the rail network on one of the busiest days of the year. With industrial action on West Midlands Trains, many passengers are left high and dry, and it's down to Network Rail to deliver the bad news(n)

programme.title: Out of Their Skin
programme.desc: Part one of two. Ian Wright charts the inspiring rise of black footballers in Britain, from Viv Anderson's debut as England's first senior black player in 1978, with the aid of archive footage, original interviews and a compilation of individual stories from the past and present. Contributors include Paul Ince, Ashley Cole, Les Ferdinand, John Barnes, Raheem Sterling and Marcus Rashford(n)

programme.desc: Ray explores the Flinders mountains and encounters some of the unusual wildlife that lives there, including three species of kangaroo and Australia's largest bird of prey(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: A family of five spanning three generations answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Hosted by Warwick Davis(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Brian, Ravi, Vanessa and Shanice pit their wits against a ruthless quiz genius in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Pollard convinces Brenda to dispose of any incriminating evidence, Rhona worries about Moira's financial situation, and the pair open up to each other, while the chemistry sizzles between Priya and Al(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Geoff feigns illness to sabotage Yasmeen's plans, Seb pledges his heart to Emma, and a reckless David places himself in harm's way(n)

programme.title: The Coronavirus Report
programme.desc: The lastest on the Covid-19 outbreak(n)

programme.title: St Davids - Britain's Smallest City
programme.desc: Documentary about the people who live and work in one of west Wales' most famous and historic communities - the city of St Davids in Pembrokeshire, which has fewer than 2,000 residents. An aeroplane pilot is forced to make an emergency landing on Whitesands beach, while the National Trust wardens try to round up some wild ponies. The men and women go head to head at the Penknife Club fishing competition(n)

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: Andrew is pushed to the edge and as he runs out of options, he concocts a plan to take drastic measures. But first, he will need help. In the present day, a vital piece of evidence comes to light and Laura seeks to discover its origin. Now in a desperate position, she must use all her wits to get the information she needs, but with Katy and Liam's assistance, she is led to a startling new suspect. Thriller, starring Joanne Froggatt and Ioan Gruffudd, with Zoe Tapper, Richie Campbell and Katherine Kelly(n)

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents(n)

programme.title: Robbie Lyle - Football Fans Under Their Skin
programme.desc: Arsenal fan Robbie Lyle looks at racism directed at football supporters, revealing how he has been abused and meeting others speaking out for the first time about their experiences of racism in football - both in the past and today(n)

programme.title: Out of Their Skin
programme.desc: Part two of two. Ian Wright charts the inspiring rise of black footballers in Britain through archive footage, original interviews and a compilation of individual stories from the past and present. Contributors include Ashley Cole, Raheem Sterling, John Barnes, Paul Ince, Rhian Brewster and Gareth Southgate(n)

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Action from the latest edition of the weekly AEW event, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley(n)

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef heads to Normandy and sets up his mobile kitchen in one of the many calvados orchards in the area, making a dish of artichokes with hollandaise. He also visits restaurant Le Pavé d'Auge in Beuvron-en-Auge to meets a chef who likes to cook with as much butter as he does(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring everything from giddy grandmas, cute kids and crazy animals to below-the-belt mishaps, practical jokers and painful-looking accidents(n)

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter chooses six of her favourite and most accessible family treks from across the UK, in search of the perfect scenic walk that anyone can manage. She begins by heading to one of the most famous landmarks on the Jurassic Coast - Old Harry Rocks in Dorset, which boasts spectacular views towards the Isle of Wight and of the offshore chalk stacks, good opportunities for bird and butterfly watching and a rich variety of wild flowers in spring and summer. There are also miles of golden sandy beaches and a dramatic vista of the bay(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: An international cycling competition comes to the Midsomer village of Burwood Mantle, but when the race leader is murdered, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson must investigate. During the race's finale, the victim overtook his team-mate to win, leaving the team owner annoyed that he disobeyed his orders, and the rival manager disappointed that his team lost. However, as the pair look into the case, they find themselves drawn into a competitive world of blackmail, bribery and bloodshed, where winning comes at any cost(n)

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents(n)

programme.title: Kate & Koji
programme.desc: Six-part series following Kate, a working-class woman who runs an old-fashioned cafe in a seaside town, as she develops a strong, if sometimes volatile, friendship with an asylum-seeking African doctor, Koji. With Kate and Koji's undercover clinic in full swing, the last thing they need is a visit from Dr Radwan. Medium keeps Koji calm, and Kate cooks up a surefire way to get back at an adversary. Comedy, starring Brenda Blethyn, Jimmy Akingbola, Blake Harrison, Barbara Flynn and Victor McGuire(n)

programme.title: The Trouble with Maggie Cole
programme.desc: Maggie is horrified that her comments about Roxanna have been interpreted by her Polish father Emil as racist, and she is relieved when they understand what she actually did say. Peter has to contend with a campaign for his resignation, as journalists try to dig up his family skeletons. Meanwhile, money lenders Patrick and Phil put the pressure on Alex to repay his debts, and Marcus and Jill's relationship blossoms when he accompanies her to the planning meeting at the village hall for the village celebrations(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
